dialogue.utterance: Hi, I'm Justin, a paramedic. What's your main concern today?
I've been having severe abdominal pain for the last few hours.
You're alert and coherent. Please tell me your name.
Tiffany. I can tell you where I am and what's happening.
Your airway is patent and unobstructed.
Your breathing is rapid at 22 breaths per minute but without visible distress.
Your pulse is strong and regularly tachycardic at 103.
You mentioned a history of pancreatitis. When was your last episode?
I was in bed resting when it suddenly came on. It feels different than previous flare-ups.
The pain seems all over and your abdomen is firm to the touch. Any nausea or vomiting?
I haven't eaten for about 12 hours.
You take prednisone, right? No other medications.
No known drug allergies, and I haven't had alcohol or anything unusual recently.
Your heart rate is still 103, recorded as sinus tachycardia on the monitor.
Breathing has slowed slightly to 20 breaths per minute, with symmetric chest expansion.
Blood pressure is 179 over 112. We'll monitor it closely.
Your oxygen saturation is 83% on ambient air.
There's no indication to check glucose at this time.
Placing a 4-lead monitor now to assess for cardiac concerns.
Starting you on 2 L NC oxygen to improve comfort and oxygenation.
I'll attempt to get an IV in your arm now for possible access.
First IV attempt unsuccessful. We won't be trying again at this time.
Pupils are equal, round, and reactive. No evidence of jugular venous distension.
Your nose and mouth appear normal. No obstruction or drainage noted.
Your lung sounds are clear and equal in both fields. Chest expansion is symmetrical.
Your abdomen is tender diffusely with mild distension. No visible signs of bleeding.
Your legs and back: skin is warm, dry, and dry tissue noted. Equal pulses and no swelling.
Urine is of normal color. No current complaints of difficulty or urgency with urination.
Skin is intact without rashes, abrasions, or scars. Appears healthy.
Can you describe the pain in more detail? Sharp, like a knife twist?
Sharp and very intense. I'd say it's a ten out of ten.
What time did the pain first appear? Tiffany: I was asleep around early evening.
Does the pain travel to your back, shoulders, or other areas?
It stays in my belly but becomes worse with movement or pressure.
How long has it been constant without any relief?
It has not stopped. Worse than usual episodes at full intensity.
I will now help you sit to a semi-Fowler's position for transport safety and comfort.
You will be secured with restraints for safety during the transport. No shortness of breath reported now.
We will transport you to [COMPANY_NAME] for further evaluation.
You will remain in a stable transport condition during this transfer, in a cold environment.
All patient notes and equipment will be handed off to the hospital RN upon arrival.
You were oxygenated, examined, and transported with equipment in place.
I assisted you onto the cot with minimal assistance, no complications.
This case is managed under medical protocol 3-2 for abdominal pain.
Your BP is still 168/97 now with no rapid changes seen leading up.
Heart rate remains 103 in sinus rhythm.
Oxygen saturation slightly improved to 89% but still low.
No chest pain or breathing difficulties reported currently.
Hospital bed 31 has been assigned for receiving care once we arrive.
ED care will continue with further diagnostic evaluation as necessary.
No unfinished interventions or reportables remain for action.
We'll complete any handover documents directly to your receiving team at the hospital.
All EMS documentation will be turned over at the hospital.
Hospital ED team will assist with your next steps upon arrival.
No changes in condition, and movement is secure during transport.
